title: Realm Climate & Land
description: One terse gazetteer line on a realm's weather and ground — written almanac-style, for rollable realm entries.
entries: Continental and hard; killing frosts arrive early, and the high passes close from first snow to thaw.
Mild and wet; the lowlands flood every spring, and every bridge has a toll and a ferryman waiting for it to wash out.
Dry uplands cut by green river valleys; all wealth and all quarrels sit within a mile of water.
Maritime; fogs off the cold current shroud the coast a hundred mornings a year, and wreckers know it.
Short fierce summers and long dark winters; the granaries are public property and guarded like treasuries.
Monsoon-fed; half the year dust, half the year deluge, and the calendar of war observes the rains.
Temperate and fat; three harvests in good years, which is why every neighbor has tried to take it at least once.
High steppe, all wind and grass; distance is measured in days of riding, not miles.
Volcanic soils under a smoking cone; the farms are rich, the towns are rebuilt often, and nobody builds in stone twice.
Cold marshland; the causeways are older than the realm, and whoever holds them holds the country.
Sun-hammered scrub and salt pans; wells are named, inherited, and killed over.
Dense old forest with farmed clearings; the roads go around the deep woods, and the deep woods are left alone.
Island weather, changeable by the hour; every farmer is half a sailor and every sailor is a weather-prophet.
Chalk downs and sweet grass; wool country, with all the money and all the smugness that implies.
Glacier-fed valleys; the rivers run milk-white and ice-cold, and drownings are counted a natural death.
Terraced hill country; a thousand years of labor holds the soil on the slopes, and terrace law is the oldest law.
Karst country — sinkholes, caves, and rivers that vanish underground; maps are guesses and locals like it that way.
Windswept moor and peat bog; fuel is cut, stacked, and taxed, and the bog keeps what it takes.
River delta, endlessly re-drawn; the border shifts with the channels, which keeps the surveyors and the armies busy.
Alpine; the realm is a chain of valleys that meet only at market and at war.
Warm and pestilent lowlands ringed by healthy hills; the rich summer uphill, the poor endure the fevers.
Prairie under an enormous sky; hail, fire, and locusts are the three named enemies, ahead of any neighbor.
A rain-shadow land, dry as bone behind its mountains; irrigation ditches are maintained by conscript labor.
Tidal flats miles wide; the sea arrives at a walking pace, and the flats claim carts, herds, and the unwary every year.
Cedar-clad mountains dropping straight into a deep cold sea; the realm is long, thin, and knit by boats.
Gentle wine country; the frost line decides which families are rich, and it moves.
Ash-grey badlands broken by oases of startling green; travel is by known stages or not at all.
Permafrost tundra; the dead are kept until the spring thaw for burial, and the spring festival is also a funeral.
Hot springs and geysers throughout; bathhouses are civic buildings, and boiling a criminal is a remembered, if retired, punishment.
Hurricane coast; the building code is scripture, and the year is counted from the last great storm.
Rolling loess hills that gully at a hard rain; the realm terraces, plants, and prays.
A single great lake moderates everything; lake-effect snows bury the shore towns and feed the best soil in the realm.
Mangrove coast and brackish channels; the realm's cavalry is a fleet of shallow-draft punts.
Bright, dry, and thin-aired on a high plateau; lowland visitors gasp for a week and sleep for two.
Mist-belt cloud forest; everything rots, rusts, or blooms, usually all three at once.
Four honest seasons in even measure; the almanac is trusted, the weather-wizards are unemployed, and the realm counts this its greatest blessing.
